extraction.summary: The video lists countries consuming the most and least amounts of various fruits worldwide.

segments.speech: The country that eats the most strawberries in the world is the United States.
The country that eats the least strawberries in the world is Afghanistan.
The country that eats the most apples in the world is China.
The country that eats the least apples in the world is Somalia.
The country that eats the most oranges in the world is Spain.
The country that eats the least oranges in the world is Mongolia.
The country that eats the most mangoes in the world is India.
The country that eats the least mangoes in the world is Norway.
The country that eats the most avocados in the world is Mexico.
The country that eats the least avocados in the world is Iceland.

manipulation_signals: rapid sequence of compilation clips from unrelated viral creators

verdicts.reasoning: China is the largest consumer of strawberries in the world, accounting for roughly 40% of global strawberry consumption, as virtually all of its domestic production (over 3-4 million metric tons annually) is consumed locally. The United States ranks second in overall consumption.

evidence.title: The Country That Produces The Most Strawberries Isn't The US

verdicts.reasoning: The claim originates from viral social media trivia lists that assign arbitrary country rankings for fruit consumption. Official agricultural figures from Afghanistan's Ministry of Agriculture, Irrigation, and Livestock show that Afghanistan produced over 600 metric tons of strawberries per year (including 681 metric tons in 2025 and 622 metric tons in 2024), all of which was absorbed by its domestic market. Because numerous other countries produce virtually no strawberries and import negligible amounts, Afghanistan is not the lowest consumer of strawberries in the world.

evidence.title: Strawberry cultivation expands in Afghanistan in 2025
Afghanistan reports 9% rise in strawberry production: Statement
Strawberries, the New Interest of Afghan Farmers

verdicts.reasoning: According to data from international agricultural reports and trade statistics, China is by far the largest consumer of apples in the world by total volume, accounting for over 50% of total global apple consumption (consuming over 30 to 40 million metric tons annually).

evidence.title: Global Apple Market in 2024-2025: Overview, Trends, and Insights

verdicts.reasoning: The claim that Somalia consumes the least apples in the world stems from viral social media trivia content, which lacks authoritative sourcing and directly contradicts other similar lists naming countries like Chad, Niger, or Indonesia. Official agricultural databases, such as those maintained by the Food and Agriculture Organization (FAO) of the United Nations, do not publish a definitive ranking for the world's lowest apple consumption.

Spain is a major producer and the world's top exporter of fresh oranges, but it does not consume the most oranges in the world. In terms of total consumption, countries such as China, Brazil, India, and the United States consume far greater volumes of oranges. In terms of per-capita consumption, countries like Brazil and Mexico rank significantly higher than Spain.

evidence.title: Fresh Oranges Fresh Domestic Consumption by Country
Oranges and mandarins, two key categories

verdicts.reasoning: The claim that Mongolia consumes the least oranges in the world appears primarily in viral social media 'fruit facts' videos, which frequently present contradictory rankings (variously naming Mongolia, Iceland, Afghanistan, or Chad as having the lowest consumption). Official agricultural and food consumption tracking organizations, such as the Food and Agriculture Organization (FAOSTAT) and the United States Department of Agriculture (USDA), do not rank Mongolia as having the world's lowest orange consumption.

India is both the world's largest producer and consumer of mangoes. Producing around 24 to 28 million metric tons annually (accounting for roughly 40% to 50% of total global production), India exports less than 1% of its harvest, with the vast majority consumed domestically.

evidence.title: 'We Will Not Share': India Exporting 1% Of Its Mango Gets Gold Memes Online

verdicts.reasoning: The claim that Norway consumes the least mangoes in the world is false. Agricultural and import trade data show that Norway regularly imports thousands of metric tons of mangoes annually from countries like Brazil, Peru, and Pakistan to meet domestic demand. Many nations with lower purchasing power, stricter import restrictions, or limited trade connections consume far fewer mangoes both in total volume and per capita. The claim appears to originate from unsourced, viral social media trivia videos that frequently share contradictory rankings.

Mexico is the leading consumer of avocados by total volume in the world, consuming around 1.3 million metric tons of avocados annually, closely followed by the United States. While the Dominican Republic leads in per-capita avocado consumption, Mexico consumes the highest total quantity overall.

evidence.title: The Country That Consumes The Most Avocados In The World

verdicts.reasoning: The claim that Iceland consumes the least avocados in the world is incorrect. Iceland imports over 1,000 metric tons of avocados annually for its population of under 400,000, yielding a per-capita consumption rate of roughly 2.5 to 3 kg per person per year. This places Iceland among the higher consumers of avocados per capita in Europe, whereas many countries in Asia, Eastern Europe, and Africa consume significantly less or virtually no avocados.

evidence.title: The European market potential for avocados
Revealed: Which European Countries Eat the Most Avocados
